codeReview.summary: Fail. The reviewed code covers the per-call `RunOptions` override flow, plain/streamed `call_model_input_filter` behavior, and structured-output `max_turns` exhaustion, but the max-turn recovery path is not parity-complete for structured-output agents. When `run_error_handlers.max_turns` returns terminal output, the runner only rejects `null`; it does not validate the recovered payload against the agent's structured output schema before accepting it. That means a structured-output run can silently succeed with invalid handler output even though normal model responses are schema-checked and the upstream parity target validates this path.

issues.description: `validate_handler_final_output()` only checks for `Value::Null`, so the `max_turns` recovery path in `crates/agents-core/src/run.rs:851` accepts arbitrary handler output for structured-output agents without schema validation. A malformed handler payload can therefore bypass the agent's declared output schema and produce a successful terminal result, which breaks structured-output parity on max-turn recovery.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The runtime worker skill requires one manual shell verification for public-facade-visible behavior, but this feature's transcript/handoff shows only automated cargo commands even though facade semantics were part of the verified surface.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/rust-runtime-worker/SKILL.md:28` requires "Perform one manual shell verification when behavior is user-visible from the public facade". The feature handoff at `/Users/staticpayload/.factory/missions/a79cf586-bde6-417e-92a4-dc7681913eac/handoffs/2026-04-16T14-43-12-164Z__runtime-turn-controls-and-input-filtering__89a1662a-5146-4312-90c2-e6c9e5aa6a93.json:14-58` lists only `cargo test`/`cargo check` commands and no manual verification entry.

summary: Reviewed the mission feature entry, handoff, transcript skeleton, commit `59fc07be53d5075a6e8257642739841902209543`, and the relevant runtime/facade sources. The feature fails review because max-turn handler recovery does not validate structured-output handler payloads against the agent schema, allowing invalid terminal outputs to succeed.
